§6Coker Gas§f

§aGaseous byproduct of light-fluid coking — essential for weapon steel and reformate gas production!§f

Coker Gas is a flammable, combustible, polluting gas with a gaseous fuel grade, providing §e62.5 kTU§f or §e93.7 kHE§f per bucket. It is produced as a byproduct when coking lighter fluids such as Wood Oil, Aromatic Hydrocarbons, Sour Gas, and various cracked/light fractions in the §bCoker Unit§f.

§6Production§f:
§b• Coker Unit§f (select recipes):
  §b–§f Wood Oil (§e3,000mB§f) → Charcoal + §e300mB§f Coker Gas.
  §b–§f Aromatic Hydrocarbons (§e1,700mB§f) → Coke + §e170mB§f Coker Gas.
  §b–§f Sour Gas (§e1,000mB§f) → Sulfur + §e150mB§f Coker Gas.
  §b–§f Cracked Diesel, Cracked Light Oil, Light Oil → smaller yields.
  §b–§f Reformate, BTX → §e10-13mB§f Coker Gas per operation.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Rotary Furnace§f: §e100mB§f Coker Gas + Steel Ingot + Flux → §e2×§f Steel Ingots. This is the primary route for §bWeapon Steel§f production.
§b• Fractioning Tower§f: §e100mB§f → §e25mB§f Aromatic Hydrocarbons + §e75mB§f Carbon Dioxide.
§b• Pyrolysis Oven§f: §e4,000mB§f → §e100mB§f Reformate Gas.

§eTip§f: Coker Gas is one of the few renewable sources of §bReformate Gas§f outside of Vacuum Refinery desulfurized crude processing. Stockpile it from your coker and process it through the Pyrolysis Oven for a steady Reformate Gas supply!
